title: Complexity Classes, Reductions, and Feasibility Boundaries · Structure Brief: Feasibility Communication
summary: Explain why a requested exact optimizer needs changed scope or a bounded alternative. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa2 feasibility communication increment in a feasibility and reduction decision memo. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain computational limits with options and evidence rather than jargon or certainty.
Choose exact bounded, parameterized, approximation, or heuristic responses from constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
